management_notes:
Marathon Gold Corporation (TSX: MOZ) was acquired by Calibre Mining Corp. on January 24, 2024, through a court-approved plan of arrangement. Former Marathon shareholders received 0.6164 Calibre Mining shares per Marathon share held. All outstanding Marathon shares are now owned by Calibre Mining and the company no longer has independent management or directors.

Marathon's common shares were delisted from the Toronto Stock Exchange at the close of business on January 26, 2024. As of that date, MOZ ceased to exist as a standalone publicly-traded entity. This score of 1 does not reflect negatively on Marathon Gold's former management team — it reflects the structural impossibility of applying the Verdict Framework to a company that no longer exists independently.

Investors who held Marathon shares at the time of acquisition received 0.6164 Calibre Mining (TSX: CXB) shares per Marathon share. Calibre Mining Corp. is the successor entity and continues to trade on the TSX under the ticker CXB.
geology_notes:
Marathon Gold's primary asset was the Valentine Gold Project in Newfoundland and Labrador, Canada — the largest permitted open-pit gold development project in Atlantic Canada at the time of acquisition. The project features multiple gold deposits aligned along a 20km structural corridor in central Newfoundland.

The Valentine Gold Project had received environmental assessment approval and was advancing toward a construction decision when Calibre Mining announced its acquisition offer in November 2023. Marathon had completed substantial technical work including a feasibility study, giving the project high geological confidence. This geology score of 3 reflects solid but not exceptional project quality — conventional open-pit deposits in a well-understood geological setting.

This geological assessment is now historical. The Valentine Gold Project is 100% owned by Calibre Mining (TSX: CXB), which was targeting approximately 500,000 oz/year combined production across its Americas portfolio. Investors seeking exposure to this asset must evaluate Calibre Mining directly.
capital_notes:
Marathon Gold no longer has an independent capital structure. All Marathon common shares were exchanged for Calibre Mining shares at a ratio of 0.6164 Calibre shares per Marathon share. Upon closing, Calibre issued an aggregate of 249,813,422 Calibre shares to former Marathon shareholders.

The acquisition valued Marathon at an implied C$0.84 per share, or approximately C$345M on a fully-diluted basis, representing the realized M&A exit for Marathon shareholders. Prior to the acquisition, Calibre had also completed a C$40M strategic investment in Marathon at C$0.60/share.

Score: 1 — Not applicable. The company has no independent capital structure. Any capital structure analysis must be directed at Calibre Mining (TSX: CXB).
catalyst_notes:
Marathon Gold as a standalone ticker has no catalysts. The company was delisted from the TSX on January 26, 2024, and all corporate activity related to the Valentine Gold Project is now reported through Calibre Mining Corp. (TSX: CXB).

Calibre Mining has disclosed production ramp plans for the Valentine Gold Mine as part of its combined operations, with production expected to contribute significantly to its target of approximately 500,000 oz/year across its Americas portfolio. These catalysts are attributable to CXB, not MOZ.

Score: 1 — Not applicable. The ticker MOZ is non-functional for investment purposes.
acquisition_notes:
The acquisition of Marathon Gold by Calibre Mining was completed at C$0.84/share implied value on a fully-diluted basis (approximately C$345M total). This is a concluded historical transaction, not an actionable investment opportunity.

The completed transaction price serves as a data point for comparable gold developer acquisitions in Atlantic Canada: the C$345M deal value for an advanced-stage, permitted project was considered reasonable at the prevailing gold price at the time (late 2023, gold approximately US$2,000/oz). At current gold prices, the asset would likely command a significantly higher valuation.

Score: 1 — The acquisition has been completed. MOZ cannot be purchased on any exchange. This score does not reflect the underlying asset quality.
analyst_summary:
VERDICT: AVOID — Marathon Gold (TSX: MOZ) is no longer a publicly traded company. Calibre Mining Corp. completed its acquisition of all outstanding Marathon shares on January 24, 2024, and MOZ was formally delisted from the Toronto Stock Exchange on January 26, 2024. Former Marathon shareholders received 0.6164 Calibre Mining (TSX: CXB) shares per Marathon share held as exchange consideration.

The composite score of 7/25 reflects the structural impossibility of applying the Verdict Framework's five factors to a delisted, acquired company — not a negative assessment of Marathon Gold's underlying assets. The Valentine Gold Project in Newfoundland and Labrador was an advanced-stage, fully permitted, feasibility-complete gold development project that was a legitimate and significant asset. The acquisition at C$345M fully-diluted was a completed M&A transaction, not a distressed outcome.

Investors interested in exposure to the Valentine Gold Project and Calibre Mining's Americas gold production portfolio should research Calibre Mining Corp. (TSX: CXB), which now owns 100% of the Valentine Gold Mine and was targeting approximately 500,000 oz/year combined production for 2025-2026. The research queue should be updated to remove MOZ and add CXB if Calibre Mining coverage is desired.
